channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Why hasn't cryptocurrency technology been adopted yet? — 46 replies — 78.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why hasn't cryptocurrency technology been adopted yet?

Open thread • Replies: 46 • Updated: 2026-06-02 08:28 UTC

OP

Why hasn't cryptocurrency technology been adopted yet? Allow me to explain.
Cryptocurrencies support decentralized networks, which are used for data computation and storage. These networks are cheaper to use than traditional server infrastructure.
If it's cheaper, why don't corporations use them? One, there are regulations preventing adoption, but CLARITY will change this in the US. Two, corporations have invested trillions into building and maintaining server infrastructure. Liquidation is risky from there point of view because they don't understand decentralized networks. A few brave corporations will lead this great transition, and when they are successful, everyone will follow.
You are in the final window of being early. Don't fuck this up.

Early replies (first 3)

62292772
>>62292765
cope

62292786
>>62292765
How much does 1 Gigabyte on the Bitcoin Blockchain cost?

62292794
>>62292786
>thinks a blockchain is the only data structure for distributed storage

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62296045
>>62296032
You are an old man, history will bury you...

62296210
>>62296032
lol

62296324
>>62292765
it took decades for the internet to really be adopted and it still hasn't been fully realized

62296340
>>62295887
Incredible amounts of money over time and it applies to the little guys even more than the big guys. Volume does come through little guys though and they will be included.
Biggest boon in decades for online and local business is coming.

62296419
>>62292827
I am calling it, clarity act will pass, despite the bots online telling you it won't. The BTC halving is under 2 years away, they're squeezing the stock market right now and then smart money rotates elsewhere to make tenbaggers.
All Congress and US politicians are bought by Israel, if you aren't profiting off of this than shame on you. Everyone across the internet will tell you crypto is over forever, and the clarity act won't pass.
Meanwhile look how much politicians own stock in MARA or MSTR. Even trump owns these two.
MARA only owns two assets by the way, bitcoin and Kaspa (Jewish trilemma solving nu coin)

62296587
>>62296419
>then my real life can begin

62296607
>>62292765
Crypto will take many decades to be adopted, and several centuries to become dominant, so we're as early as possible.

We're the bastards that were around when BTC was only $73,000.

62296629
>>62292765
In order to use somebody else's compute, you need to compensate them fairly for it. The math will never work when you consider the price advantage large companies have when buying hardware at scale. Also, they technically already do decentralization with CDNs.

62296638
>>62292765
It doesnt offer a solution to a problem that exists.

62297373
>>62296629
For a huge amount of the business world, actual compute isn't even the real issue. It's interconnects. Data movement. When you're breaking up a 10 terabyte job across a thousand nodes, you are very very rarely bottlenecked by compute. You're bottlenecked by shuffling the data between machines. The whole reason massive datacenters exist is because they can use insane interconnects (1TB, even 10 TB/s fabric). Using retarded internet shitcoin routing mechanisms to attempt this is like using a garden hose to put out a lava flow.

62297920
>>62296638
t. isn't in business and has no money

62297957
>>62296032
i think your missing the point of the tech

62298066
>>62296629
>>62297373
There are P2P relay schemes for efficient data flow. Kademlia routing is one example.

62298770
>>62292765
It has been adopted. Not on a grand scale though. Bitcoin is a deflationary store of value. XMR to buy drugs on the darknet. Stablecoins are used. Perpdex as an online gambling platforms.

62301398
>>62295608
>2028? more like 2030 if the hype bubble pops first

62301403
>>62297373
>implying some shitcoin meshnet can replace infiniband

lmao, anon doesn't understand why dc exist

62301409
>>62297957
the main thing missing here is the practical tradeoff, not the headline claim

62302054
>>62297957
Anyone invoking "the tech" is almost certainly stupid. There is exactly one piece of useful tech in crypto:
- the idea of a distributed ledger than is extremely hard to forge or alter

That's it. Everything else, which at this point is about 95% of the gibberish coming from your scamming overlords is pure nonsense. Bait to keep you on the hook because it makes you feel smart and in-the-know.

Just think about the whole idea of a "trusted oracle". How the fuck is a trusted oracle going to provide some techno-based unbiased ground truth for "this company failed to complete grouting my bathroom properly"? I'll give you a hint: there's huge huge companies that already do this. They're called insurance companies with vast numbers of field employees who investigate insurance claims. And millions of small-claims lawyers. There's no way to do it other than physical inspection. An infallible "oracle" is absolute nonsense for probably 99% of all 'contracts' in the world.

62302089
>>62302054
Holy shit, you're dumb.

62302097
>>62302089
The thing about you crypto fags is that you never actually attempt to rebut anything. You just instantly go to the boomer-esque insults with nothing else. It's like any boomer politics forum, but for mutt zoomers and failson millenials.

62302126
>>62302097
Here's some rebuttal for you.
A ledger is one of many applications for distributed networks.
You don't understand what oracles are. You understand what some YouTuber without a computer science background said about oracles.

62302152
>>62302126
An oracle is a trusted service that can provide the inputs needed to satisfy a smart contract, no? The question becomes how exactly it can verify these inputs for anything other than very large scale questions, like the standard hurr-durr rainfall/crop-insurance example.

t. computer scientist btw

62302300
>>62292765
> If it's cheaper, why don't corporations use them?

Because corporations want power and control over things. They want to centralize it in their hands.

62302324
>>62302300
Stupid. Cloud compute is an enormous business. AWS, GCP, etc. Companies use them because it's cheaper than setting up their own hardware these days. COST is the key. If your stupid shitcoin mesh compute network was anywhere near as cost effective they'd be using it.

62302421
>>62302324
AWS/GCP isnt decentralized. It has authority that controls it.

62302431
>>62302421
Sure, but you said "corporations want power and control over things". The number of corporations who USE cloud compute is 10000x higher than the number of corporations who provide cloud compute. So, clearly, those 10000x corporations don't really care about control. If they did, they'd have their own hardware, right? They just want to run some ad-analysis jobs as cheaply as possible. So they choose the cloud providers, who have assembled insanely powerful clusters that are easy to use.

62302440
>>62302431
And just so you rubes have some sort of idea of the scale of "big data", here's an article about Amazon's S3 service that used to send physical trucks with 100 PETABYTE capacity to places that needed to upload their shit to AWS.

https://www.datacenterdynamics.com/en/news/aws-retires-snowmobile-truck-based-data-transfer-service/

And they RETIRED that shit because it wasn't even enough!. Your duplo block shitcoin mesh compute scheme isn't even a fart in the wind compared to this.

62302497
>>62302054
>>62302152
You're conflating "inputting" data into smart contracts with actually sourcing data.
Oracles are very rarely the data source itself, they merely take the data source selected by a user and put it on chain securely.

>muh bathroom grout
Who the fuck cares what data the user wants, if they want bathroom grout data then that's what they want and they will have to pay for it. People want strange shit sometimes.

62302543
>>62292794
Then show the costs of maintaining non-blockchain decentralized network vs just maintaining your own server. Oh wait you can't because security costs of something with so much open volatility are impossible to predict.

62302568
>>62296032
It could be cheaper for the companies themselves who offload all their hardware costs and energy demands onto idealistic cucks like the anon you are arguing with, but the main problem is security.

item.title: What’s stopping you from buying BTC? — 160 replies — 42.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
What’s stopping you from buying BTC?

Open thread • Replies: 160 • Updated: 2026-06-02 05:16 UTC

OP

What is it? Fear? Lack of conviction? Waiting for lower prices? Or do you genuinely think Bitcoin fails long term?

Curious what’s holding people back at this stage.

Early replies (first 3)

62271721
>>62271712
Btc needs a catalyst. It probably will be yield curve control with a new name. It could also be codifying the SBR into law which is less likely. Or my personal favorite schizo theory, it's discovered that Iran was mining and selling bitcoin to fund it's endeavors and that their supply is finally exhausted.

62271922
>>62271712
Gays in it.

62271933
>>62271712
everybody and their femboy lovers above two-digits IQ are waiting for BTC to dump below at least 40k by July. Why anybody buy now? 4 years cycles never lie

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62288112
>>62273994
So it's not worth it?

62288281
>>62271712
Indifference. I got into the market when the BTC was $3K. but I bought Link instead. Pleased with my decision. I respect what it has done and continues to do with the world but I get excited for smart contracts more, which the BTC doesn't have.

62288653
>>62271712
Because I got caught with my pants down avgcost 95k, and I've been trimming it and using that money as play dump money. So far the 15k I sold from Btc and put into Sandisk have turned in 110k already, and the 10k into gold are at 14k, and it's an actual asset. Never buying that retarded shit again. I still have like 30k and maybe I'll keep that, but chances are I'll probably sell and ride the SpaceX pump and dumo with it. Put in 50k into it, sell 50k when it doubles in price, keep the rest as gains. Did the same shit with Micron, Sandisk and silver, take profits and just keep the rest. This is a hype based market and btc has no hype anymore.

62290061
>>62288112
We'll find out when TBVs actually launch.
Then you can judge the product based on what it does, not on what random people online tell you to think.

62290076
>>62275451
Ok, just buy altcoins

62290078
>>62272002
ahahahahahahahah

62290085
>>62275501
If BTC goes to zero, alts are probably even more cooked.

62290480
>>62287464
If it's such an obvious scam, why are you still here watching it?

62290856
>>62271933
There's truth to this, but I dunno about 40k. Buy "dips" now I say

62290931
>>62272803
>>62272874

The conundrum that niggers on here don't understand is that everyone wants upside volatility, but it's the volatility itself that ultimately creates the ceiling.

It's the cute naive chick that ceases to be so once you fuck her. You can't have your cute and fuck it too.

62291823
>>62290931
You're also another nigger

62292614
>>62287393
So how much are you making?

62292863
>>62271712
the absolute best case scenario for bitcoin would be a 3x. there are other coins currently on the market that can and probably will give me a higher roi.

62292885
>>62271712
Someday a scalable bitcoin will fork away from BTC with enough ideological support to effectively upgrade what everyone will agree to be bitcoin. Then I will buy bitcoin. Until then it's pointless to buy a coin just to get the real bitcoin eventually as a dividend fork. Just wait and buy scalable bitcoin when it exists.

62292989
>>62271712
>saylor holding 5% (more due to lost coins)
>trump stink
>american hegemony stink
>quantum fud
>couldnt even act as a hedge during a war which is its only job
>2028 gary gensler revenge tour
Wasn't the plan this whole time to dump on the institutions? The OGs dumped billions and billions of market clips on them and left forever. That was the entire goal and it was accomplished, genuinely wtf more do you want? The win-con was fulfilled, if you didn't make it then come up with a new one or find another asset. Or you can continue DCAing for the next 30 years I guess

62292997
What are you getting, a 2-4x depending on where the bottom is?

62294119
>>62292863
And what is it? Low cap alts?

62294591
>>62271960
They will say the same thing when it hit 200K Kek. Honestly BTC has a lot of potential most especially since only 1% is in Defi, Imagine how huge it will be with TBV now in play.

62295602
>>62292997
Yeah, that's it.

62295607
>>62272678
Under 20k? Are you sure?

62295716
>>62271712
>What’s stopping you from buying BTC?
the existence of Monero

62295989
>>62274282
Why the hate

62296012
>>62295607
sure under 20k
why whats your get back in number?

62297371
>>62296012
Not under 20k

62297376
>>62295716
Ah privacy ok ok

62298604
>>62294591
You're right anon

62298612
>>62292989
Well, I'm not afraid of short term volatility

62300751
>>62288653
Well, follow your plan.

62301081
>>62271712
It's not 2013, the BTC ship sailed a long time ago

62302256
>>62301081
Let's see how it goes anon

item.title: I TOLD YOU SO MUAHAHAHA >>62288629 — 19 replies — 19.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
I TOLD YOU SO MUAHAHAHA >>62288629

Open thread • Replies: 19 • Updated: 2026-06-02 08:07 UTC

OP

I TOLD YOU SO MUAHAHAHA

>>62288629

https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/crypto/articles/michael-saylors-bitcoin-treasury-firm-122216992.html

Early replies (first 3)

62298232
Bloody bastard! He made promise he wouldnt sell god fucking damnit

62298234
Anyone gets the feeling that Saylor won’t be part of the next bull run?

62298235
It's not enough, Saylor needs to be totally cleaned out for crypto to ever rise again.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62298248
I hope this retarded permabull learns from this lesson

62298253
buy high, sell low

the biz way

62298267
BTC sub 60k today

Chainlink suppression killed it

62298280
>>62298216
Kek what a retard

62298598
32 btc sold, literal nothing burger.

62298622
>>62298598
32 today, it's only the beginning, once we are sub 60k he will panic sell thousands. Screencap this.

62298715
Pretty bearish for the asset backed ponzi scheme it's having to liquidate asset instead of just diluting more.

62298807
This was announced one month ago during the earnings call:

"We'll probably sell some Bitcoin to fund a dividend just to inoculate the market—just to send the message that we did it,” he said during the Q&A.

100% of the posters in this thread so far didn't acknowledge this, which proves his point that the headline will cause a lot of anti-BTC celebrations, so it is better to get the headline out of the way when the time is right.

Company officials already stated many months ago that they might sell BTC if it is considered helpful. For example, if the company's common stock is severely undervalued, trading some of its BTC for stock is probably a good deal.

The idea that the company cannot ever possibly sell any BTC was always absurd (Saylor's early statements on this probably didn't help), and they're right to start breaking this imagined taboo because people (like the posters here) only read the headlines.

62301366
>>62298807
>Saylor's early statements on this probably didn't help
Correct. Too many this is the only thing that matters.

62301536
>>62298232
he's selling to fund STRC dividends retard. this is what keeps the flywheel going

62302510
>>62302507
if only you knew how bad things really are

62302513
>>62301536
strc scam is collapsing, he barely has enough to pay dividends for a couple months

62302528
>>62298807
they are so broke that they needed 32 BTC?

62302544
>>62302507
Did he really post this? If so, that's an interesting message.

item.title: Day 1732 of snailposting every day until BTC is at 1M — 4 replies — 1.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
Day 1732 of snailposting every day until BTC is at 1M

Open thread • Replies: 4 • Updated: 2026-06-02 06:39 UTC

OP

Day 1732 of snailposting every day until BTC is at 1M

Early replies (first 3)

62300793
>>62298321
VELY CUTE SNALE COMING THROUGH

62301568
>>62300793
How's it been going man?

62301579
Kek, you should go back to the original target of $100k

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62302430
>>62301568
started the last part of the renovation now... repairing old marble... holy shit its tedious :(
you?

item.title: HOLY. FUCK. — 15 replies — 60.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
HOLY. FUCK.

Open thread • Replies: 15 • Updated: 2026-06-02 08:56 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62301820
@grok is this legal?

62301873
>>62301796
iirc I don't think the wash sale rule applies to crypto yet, so yes you can if that is true. You might get in trouble if your dealing with a significant sum though or like sell enough to deduct your entire taxable income and your job isn't a Mcwage job, the IRS has a catch-22 called the economic substance doctrine where they could argue that you were trying to fleece them in an audit or court proceeding.

62301881
There are actively managed funds that intentionally do stuff like this to get you tax breaks every year.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62301884
>>62301873
Capital losses can only be used to offset capital gains, not wage income.

62301886
>>62301796
the IRS doesn't mind if you do this

62301897
>>62301884
Yeah you're right

62301905
>>62301820
No, it isn't. It's a wash sale unless at least a month has passed.
Besides, there are state that cap your losses for deduction to something like 3k per year. You can't report more than that.

62301920
>>62301796
Also OP You can only offset your capital gains to 3k in that case, but you can rollover beyond that I think indefinitely. Don't take this drunkies advice to heart though it's just what I remember from filling out my own tax forms

62301931
>>62301905
could probably buy back into a bitcoin etf and get away with it...wont be able to buy illicit goods anymore though

62301947
>>62301920
I thought it was unlimited against capital gains but limited against income.

62302187
>>62301947
Prettu sure it isn't. 3k a year, tops. So good luck with that 13 years writing off your shitcoin loss :)

62302188
>>62302187
not true it offsets capitalized gains

62302190
>>62302187
Nah I'm right. Capital losses have unlimited offset against capital gains as long as they are both short or long term. The $3k limit your talking about is offsetting regular income with capital losses

62302206
>>62301931
just buy WBTC lmao

62302609
>>62301884
You can deduct up to 3000 dollars a year in capital losses from your earned income

item.title: What is the lowest stress path to becoming a multimillionaire? — 39 replies — 38.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
What is the lowest stress path to becoming a multimillionaire?

Open thread • Replies: 39 • Updated: 2026-06-02 08:02 UTC

OP

What is the lowest stress path to becoming a multimillionaire? Assuming one lives in USA

Early replies (first 3)

62299698
>>62299690
Just do nothing and wait, we will all be billionaires within the next decade

62299702
>Assuming one lives in USA
the first step is to get the fuck out of that dangerous brown shithole and go to Europe

62299711
>>62299690
Inheritance

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62299894
>>62299690
unironically sports gambling

62299936
>>62299690
easiest 10 year path?

invest $20k in pokemon sealed product, typically 3-5x's every 3-4 years

$20k into $80k into $250k into $1mil

literally free money, think of it as buy having sealed product you become the casino house, older packs always in demand and just go up once out of print

62299942
>>62299795
VOO for lower stress desu

62299981
>>62299936
top signal

62299985
>>62299690
become a trophy husbando and marry some gay homosexual faggot dude or worse, marry some rich arab fugly hairy bitch

62300041
>>62299942
hasn't VGT outperformed though for like the past decade? doesn't it more sense to concentrate on tech?
>>62299936
is there any reason why the bubble wouldn't just pop this year? can you reason why it's not just a greater fool asset?
>>62299985
I'd be too miserable

62300109
>>62300041
The tech sector outperforms, but its downturn will also be much harder compared to a broader, more defensive index like VOO (hence "lower stress")

62300120
>>62299698
kek

62300255
>>62299690
$1x2x2x2x2x2x2x2x2x2x2x2x2x2x2x2x2x2x2x2x2
Basically you can start with $1 and keep finding assets that just double (2x)
Is it likely you choose the correct asset 20 times in a row? Maybe not, or if you do it will involve a lot of reading and educating yourself to make correct choices + waiting for that 2x to happen.
But realistically you can start with more than just $1 in one single asset. $10 put into multiple assets that are below $1 (or really any price since the 2x is the real prize).
Speaking of realism, it's unlikely you ONLY pick assets that 2x, never choosing something that does a 3x, 5x, 10x, etc. I've spotted assets that did 30x in a year, dips that then did 10,000x.
There's also plenty of stuff I don't see because I'm not looking around with every hour of free time I have. I never started as low as $1, I almost always did $100 to $1000.
It really is easier than it seems because it's just one LONG process of educating yourself to make good choices. College/uni/trades/certifications are also A LOT of money + long hours of education in the hopes of making good choices on the tests. Then you hope there are job openings in your chosen field by time you're done, or you can move somewhere that has openings, or people will accept you for another field since you're proven you are a capable learner.
The thing is most people just never try, then many of the ones who do get complacent/greedy and lose hundreds of thousands. You could say endurance is the real "difficult" part, but don't get impatient. Be diligent and enjoy watching your earnings multiply over time.
Expect it to take a while, but since you don't control how high an asset goes when it pops-off it really might not take as long as you think. Better to be diligent about looking for opportunities and researching an asset's potential and if something does a crazy 100x, cool. If not it's still chill since that wasn't your expectation anyway.

62300263
>>62299690
Invest 333k into the s&p and wait 20 years

62300277
>>62299716
True, I'm up 50k with no effort from doing this.

62300285
>>62299702
oh another brown shithole. no thanks

62300337
>>62300041
You're miserable now, husbandmaxx your way into billions and financial security. You can divorce rape your troon husband afterwards

62300358
>>62299690
For me it's starting a business and hiring other people to do all the work

That would probably stress you out though if you've never done it

62300429
>>62299795
50/50 of nasdaq and snp, with a 5-10% of vxus or similar. More weighting towards the largest companies is better in the long run

62300484
>>62300041
>is there any reason why the bubble wouldn't just pop this year? can you reason why it's not just a greater fool asset?
is there any reason art industry would pop this year? can you reason why thats not just a great fool asset?

62300487
>>62300484
art/luxury brands/watches etc

62300731
>>62300484
>>62300487
Idk, I don't buy those. I figure it's just money laundering.

62300903
>>62300109
I guess, but it just seems like a waste if you're holding long term anyway

62300936
>>62299690
I guess buying gold, and putting it in a bank vault. Putting say a thousand dollar each month. Do this for 20 years.

62301038
>>62300903
In a bull market, you’re absolutely right. But if a bubble pops, you’re in for long years of suffering with tech stocks.

Reminder: if you bought a NASDAQ-100 index fund at the top of the dotcom bubble in 2000, you had to wait until 2015 (!!!) to be in the green again, even with so many great companies in the index. Meanwhile the S&P 500 recovery delay was less than 7 years.

Tech is not stress-free at all when the market goes south.

62301246
>>62299690
Selling pictures of your butthole on onlyfans

62301404
>>62300731
so? just buy and make money

62301415
Actuary-maxx (boring, kind of hard but not impossible)
Live below your means and save/invest as much as you want
No more than two children

62301431
>>62299690
The most common is to inherit $2 million from grandpa.

62302214
>>62299690
Haha at the retard in this thread shilling pokemon investing. Of all the brownoid traps I have ever seen, this is right at the top.

62302274
>>62299702
I'd rather not get stabbed by browns

62302488
>>62299690
Inherit a billion dollars and blow 99% of it partying.

62302523
>>62299690
Save about $2800 a month and invest it in something safe, like an index fund. At an average return of 7%, you'll have 2mil in 20 years.

Though, inflation will eat a lot of that up, at least 40%, and your 2mil will spend like 1.2mil. Invest about $4k a month instead to get ahead of it. Its the only way surefire way.

62302537
>>62302523
most people in the US don't make $4k per month after taxes, let alone have that much left over after expenses.

item.title: crypto shills destroyed /biz/ — 54 replies — 18.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
crypto shills destroyed /biz/

Open thread • Replies: 54 • Updated: 2026-06-02 06:07 UTC

OP

you stupid shitcunts nobody is going to buy your fucken dumb digital coin that has no fucken use case

You were too late for bitcoin

you got psychologically destroyed by millennial and gen x goobers posting on twitter from their rented condos in miami

And now you're here trying to tiktok dance your way into fucken millions

GTFO

Early replies (first 3)

62293753
>>62293749
don't you have some dying boomer business to take care of lol

62293756
>>62293753
wowee

*does the fortnite dance*

feed my more low i.q. shortform slop sir!

Imagine being a GEN Z male who cannot even focus long enough to fill out a fucken business plan

62293763
>>62293756
imagine being so small brained that you have to work with dirty pipes since its the only way you wont lose everything. its indeed better you leave the crypto to smarter people and focus on the piss and shit

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62294210
>>62293749
t top buying baggie.

62294219
>>62294208
>>pokemon cards
i think you're confusing anon here, i never mentioned anything about trading cards.

62294221
>>62294219
My mistake. You are still a homosexual zoomer for investing in shitcoins.

62294231
>>62294221
wow, that was a very rude thing to say.

62294248
>>62293749
>crypto shills destroyed /biz/
/biz/ was created specifically to contain crypto shilling

62294250
>>62294194
>Chainlink solves multiple real world problems.
stopped reading right there, baggie

62294305
>>62294194
>Here's a really simple example for you: tokenized gold that's backed by physical gold. This will enable the whole world to have exposure to hard money
Explain what this means. Seriously, explain it. Tokenized gold that's backed by physical gold. How is that any different than buying 'gold' on an exchange that's just a certificate? They're both digital, but one of them is just stored on a rube-goldberg mechanism instead of a simple database. How does it enable "the whole world" to have exposure to hard money?

You're just saying fucking words.

62294322
>>62294305
>how are tokenized assets different from legacy assets??

t.

62294326
>>62294322
Yes. In a qualitative way, not a quantitative way, explain how they are fundamentally different. Being stored on a slightly different digital substrate doesn't change : you hold a -promise- that you own gold, not actual physical gold.

62294328
>>62294326
Ask your local AI fuckhead.
Nobody here wants to explain the absolute basics of tokenization to you, even the banks have been trying to explain the benefits for over half a decade.

62294331
>>62294328
I want to see you explain it, because you can't, without regurgitating another explanation that will be fille with just as much of a smokescreen.

62294337
>>62294331
listen to yourself

62294340
>>62294337
>a really simple example for you
You're the one who said it. If it's so simple, surely you can bang out a coherent explanation in a minute or two.

You're a tech fetishist. You are mentally bound to this retarded world because you imagine it makes you smart, but it's really just smart in the "grandma thinks he's good with computer" kind of way.

62294365
>>62294340
Take a deep breath you fat boomer, your bp is spiking.

62294367
>>62293749
These sluts must know they are creating wankmaterial. They arent fooling anyone. Skanks. I hate it when they trick my brain into wanting to put my penis inside them. They would be raped 24/7 in the old days.

62294368
>>62294365
I accept your concession.

62294378
>>62293749
That's not even her real body. The app program she's using starts stuttering to keep up as she turns to the side. She's probably built like a soup can.

62294385
>>62293749
use cases are bearish desu

62294446
>>62294385
Probably why memes did so well last cycle. Why i am buying blue chip memes again while they are cooked.

62294649
>>62293756
can you clean my pipe?

62294739
>>62293749
I was at a Calgary Flames hockey game last year and there was a boomer ahead of me who must have drank 7 or 8 tallboys and was just watching shit like this on his phablet.

Sometimes you gotta respect the absolute zero regard boomers have.

62296803
>>62294194
nobody wants hard money lmfao
soft money is how the system works because they can just run w/e fucken scams they want
the system isn't for the people brother
gold was done away with for this exact reason

62298686
>>62293845
if you're so smart, why aren't you rich?
turns out you're fucking stupid.

62299806
>>62293749
just dont be too late for xrp

62301355
>>62293749
anyone who after years has not taken their involvement in crypto or more broadly business beyond shitposting about tokens on a uruguayan cattle milking forum is ngmi

62301664
I'm still bagholding shitcoins that I bought 4 years ago and I'm worse off now than when I first got into crypto. I feel nothing. No hope, no emotions. I don't know wtf to do anymore in life. I hardly have the energy to go to the cage. I just want my life to end

62301988
>>62301664
Take the loss, move the money into actual investments. An index fund, whatever. Watch it grow, because it is based on physical reality (the output of the entire country). Keep contributing to it. 10 years from now it will have compounded so much you'll forget this whole thing.

Literally, just be a normie. Normies are fucking rich these days as long as they invest carefully and don't just pour everything down doordash and rent.

62302350
>>62301988
>Yes goy, sell the crypto bottom and buy the S&P top

62302354
>>62293749
XMR has more of a use case than BTC at this point

62302369
>>62302350

item.title: *continues to predict the price of BTC in your path* All you had to do was buy on green and sell on red, at least we're… — 83 replies — 16.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
*continues to predict the price of BTC in your path* All you had to do was buy on green and sell on red, at least we're…

Open thread • Replies: 83 • Updated: 2026-06-02 04:01 UTC

OP

*continues to predict the price of BTC in your path*

All you had to do was buy on green and sell on red, at least we're close to a buy signal now

prev thread:
>>62261213

Early replies (first 3)

62284885
How do I use this?

62284969
>>62284885
green = buy dips
red = sell rips
yellow = volatility likely, derisk
the site has a realtime FX-based confirmation layer - positive confirms buys and invalidates sells, negative confirms sells and invalidates buys
today FX is solidly green so early buys are defensible
chart gets updated every month with a new 4-week, next will be on Sunday

62284994
imagine buying bitcoin over 70K

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62289468
>>62284814
L, and let me be perfectly clear, FG

62289671
>>62289468
So up from here? Don’t speak zoomzoom

62289838
>>62289671
This is where price should turn up - hybrid FX+physics says Buy because FX is so positive, physics officially flips green tomorrow, it all seems pretty good for bulls over the next 24 hours.

62289954
>>62287275
this. I just go all in bottom of the bear market and do nothing a couple years

62291704
>>62284814
This is no proper backtesting! You must test at least 30 instances!
https://youtu.be/zXoZdnYfIlA?si=MPB49se3bA1H2fnp

62291711
>>62291704
*32 Instances

62291719
>>62291711
>>62291704
?

62291779
>>62291719
How big are the wins and how big are the losses? They have to be in a profitable proportion together with the win rate. 66% win rate with wins 1/3 as big as the losses is not profitable for example.

62291951
>>62284814
broken clock o algo

62292012
what time of day do you make the trades

62292563
if you have a profitable strategy then just make a hyperliquid vault so we can join it and you have 10% profit share

62292730
>>62291779
There's a whole website and twitter account where you can check these things
>>62292012
>a broken clock predicts the future 80% of the time
That's one hell of a broken clock
>>62292012
The demo strategy executes ~2 hours into NY open, when there's enough FX data to confirm or inval the signal
>>62292563
Down the list of things to do. Right now it's a trade bot, which is being built because a mid-tier CEX wants to explore white labelling signals, so the bot needs to be optimized, but then I'll absolutely integrate it into HL

62292891
>>62292730
Thank you, I already have working strategies. I was just advocating doing proper backtesting before applying any strategy.

62292918
>>62292891
>I was just advocating doing proper backtesting before applying any strategy.
Always a good practice - prediction/outcome goes back to December 2024 which is where the hit rate is from. I could backtest to mid-2024 (when the FX layer was added) if I feel like it but I figured 100+ signals over more than a year was good enough

62293541
Spoonfeed me pls OP? Am I supposed to follow your twitter? How do I get the meme lines for myself.

62293559
>>62293541
>How do I get the meme lines for myself.
I'll post them in here, but there's also a live dashboard and a twitter, where a follow/likes would be greatly appreciated
>>62285878

62293571
>>62293559
Thanks for doing the needful sir. I'll be checking this out. Big ups anon.

62293619
>>62292730
Does the trading bot have contingencies in place just in case other trading bots start countertrading your strategy if it ends up being too successful?

62293680
>>62293619
I think if someone else's trading bot can move BTC by a few percent per day then we all have much bigger problems

62294595
>>62284814
Just buy the dip and sell the high simple. The main thing is to hold. I'm not only holding, I also staked some of my BTC recently since we can do that now without bridge involved.

62296711
BEHOLD, THE FUTURE

62296738
>>62296711

62297476
AAAAAAAAAAAAAA

62298184
>>62296711
Is that you Michael?

62298251
>>62284814
>buy signal
>5/28
This aged like milk.

62299491
>>62298251
>can't read
Maybe investing is not for you anon

>>62298184
Shhh it's a secret

62301262
>>62292563
>>62292730
what's a hyperliquid vault

62301440
>tfw if I listened to the schizo chart I would be down 10%

62301542
All i can see is that you've been incorrect? Am i missing something? Lol

62302104
>>62301542
>>62301440
We'll see - the hit rate's 78%, not 100%. The general sense, per the twitter thread yesterday, is bounce to the 5th-7th, then drop to the real bottom mid-June and then pump through summer, but obviously Brownian motion can't predict Saylor selling lol

item.title: No real Bitcoiner is getting shaken out by this. — 27 replies — 7.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
No real Bitcoiner is getting shaken out by this.

Open thread • Replies: 27 • Updated: 2026-06-02 05:27 UTC

OP

Saylor sells 32 BTC out of 843,000+ BTC and anon starts acting like Bitcoin is dead.

You're not a real BTC holder.

Early replies (first 3)

62299202
why did he sell 32 btc tho?

62299205
>>62299202
to get a higher rating from some corporate wagie on wall street. he was showing his btc holdings are unencumbered and can be sold at will.

62299325
Didn't he tell people to never sell?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62299379
>>62299205
Showing that 'his holdings can be sold at will' has more downside than up.

62299533
>>62299202
He's trying to see how the market reacts. He'll drop the full bag later this week.

62299553
>>62299533
Hopefully

62299564
>>62299187
BTC is dead. Everyone is needing capital for the bull stock market
The volume is so low, there is not enough liquidity to dump 843,000 BTC

62299589
He's selling his high cost basis coins, the market is dumping and he's just going to buy them cheaper. Guys a fucking genius

62299647
>>62299589
Would have been even more genius if he hadn’t been seething against BTC when it was $200

62299824
>>62299187
hes so fucked, LUNA 2.0 inc

62299854
.

62299869
>>62299202
had to pay Sergey for ccip access

62299903
>>62299187
Cats out of the bag sandjeep

62299907
this idiot kills the market

62300165
>>62299187
dumb ass apparently buys over the counter and sells on a centralized exchange on the open market. controlled opposition nigger detected kek.

62300746
>>62299202
For some reason.

62300755
>>62299205
He can do whatever he wants tho

62300807
>>62299325
Yeah before but I don't mind

62300810
>ponzi scheme can't dilute on the back of a speculative asset anymore
Buttcoin is over.

62300991
>>62299187
This is how it starts dumbass, why sell BTC at all if he's got money? especially selling at a loss says alot.

62301317
>>62300807
I don't either, I have no skin in the game. Just funny how the poster child for btc is going back on his word.

62301382
The fact that crypto is even a thing shows how most people have zero understanding of money or value.

62301632
>>62301590
Absolute bullshit as usual.

Microsoft, Coca-Cola or Boeing don’t sell their intellectual property; it doesn’t mean this IP has no value, that it’s ignored by rating agencies, or that it’s not real.

The only person who suggested MSTR would never sell its BTC is Saylor himself; no one ever told him his BTC "can’t be sold" or prevented him from selling anything. He cornered himself into this position by his own volition.

And selling 32 BTC only proves that you had 32 BTC, nothing more. The only unquestionable method to prove your Bitcoin is "real" would be to give us the adresses of your wallets, Mike. The entire world could check the state of your BTC reserves in real time. But you never want to reveal this basic info. Why?

Lies lies and lies.

62301643
>>62301590
beta

62302272
>>62301317
I don't care who the major players are that sell their BTC. It doesn't really affect me since I'm focused on the long term. That's what I mean.

62302278
>>62299379
but every BTC holder can sell at will. That's not exactly unique.

item.title: saylor SOLD — 8 replies — 5.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
saylor SOLD

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-06-02 07:35 UTC

OP

32 today. 3200 tomorrow. mstr is over. 2026 lunc

Early replies (first 3)

62300919
we can only hope
put that micro-Tradegy out of misery

62300923
saylor just transferred 40K Bitcoin to Binance

62300944
>>62300923
ON THE DEAD HOMIES ITS OVER

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62300954
>>62300923
Is this true? Arkham link?

62301513
Welcome to the planned demolition. You know how you milk the most money cows at once? Suppress the fuck outta the coin that's actually got developers and infrastructure to handle the financial markets, pump the #1 coin, then flip it after you've set a narrative for 24 ish months. Not that I know what I'm talking about, I don't, and I'm being genuine. I'm merely being speculative. But with the interest increase from institutions, and the amount of money that goes into paying people to vet investments at that level, you're telling me that no one has evaluated the eth vs btc value proposition if even only particular markets move on-chain? No one? Nobody is talking to ethereum developers to bring the world's first stock ownership record on blockchain? I don't believe it. It's what blockchain was made for. Low transaction volume compared to currency It is a settlement layer, absolute and transparent, with an immutable public record, that thrives on transaction volumes less than currency, which this is by orders of standard deviation.

62302155
>>62301513
This guy gets it, wp dood, u r above this place, leave, unironically, this shithole is 80iq avg, at least the paid eth fudders of hyderabad have 80iq here. Scaled mainnet, 900k validators, chain is unbroken, biggest tvl, most stables, one of the best tokenomics with smaller than 1% yearly inflation, i could do this listing all day, but you know, coin price go down baad coin saaar, buy spacex!

62302422
>>62300913
68k
65k
62k
....

item.title: How is it that everyone on reddit are millionaires — 39 replies — 4.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
How is it that everyone on reddit are millionaires

Open thread • Replies: 39 • Updated: 2026-06-01 23:43 UTC

OP

>excluding crypto
Seriously, do you all have more than 200k in your brokerage accounts? Its crazy to me that you Americans are that well off. Even on ticktok live streams, you see this guy presenting his 200k+ portfolio in his mid to late 20s. Others on reddit are showcasing their portfolio at mid 30 already reaching 1M+. Yet people all around me are claiming that life has gotten increasingly harder and more expensive. Here is Switzerland we are getting drained with increasing Krankenkasse (health care) costs and mandatory radio-service billings (Serafe) to the point that we are voting to decrease these this month, somehow.
This cant all be fake personal marketing and fake boasting, can it?
Statistics show that the median savings in top wealth western nations is like 50-80k (checking, savings and investments, excluding house, cars etc.) in total.
ALSO...WHERE IS ALL THIS MONEY COMING FROM TO INVEST IN THE STOCK MARKET? How are these companies being pumped so heavily with cash that giga-corps like Micron reach 1 trillion easily overnight?

Early replies (first 3)

62295606
>>62295508
It's called consolidation. There's very few websites of any note and only a few kooks in the developed world are consciously not online so if you're rich you're gonna be in one or two communities.

62295628
>>62295508
??? it's a bull market

62295633
>>62295508
>obsessed with americans backwoods eurohillbilly
lol

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62296281
>>62295508
they live with their parents, meaning their parents are millionaires, not them

or their parents passed on some of their wealth to them. There's a lot of Gen X and boomer millionaires here since all you had to do was buy a house in a moderately popular part of the US and you're probably now a millionaire.

or the people on reddit are much older than you expect. For example I'm a millionaire and have been for 20+ years now, but I am also almost 60 years old now. People on 4chan assume I must be lying about money because they can't imagine a 60 year old boomer posting on 4chan.

62296414
>check'em
P.S. Git Gud.

62296439
>>62295737
>Switzerland
>europoor thirdie

62296451
>>62296281
Until proven otherwise, I expect the age of any poster in forums (including 4chan) or newspaper comment sections to be between 50 and 70, desu

Everything, literally everything makes sense once you read with this assumption in mind

62296456
>>62295508
Its called working and investing your money into an index fund. I know its fucking crazy but its actually easy being a millionaire if you are patient.

62296458
because stocks are on an absolute tear, and none of these zoomers are doing crypto which just takes.

62296494
>>62296458
True, tech stocks are the place for retarded money today (just left curve it bro, stop having thoughts and shit)

>if they're retarded, how come they make tons of money and you don't? lol
They're going to finish like they did in crypto in 2017-2022, and in online poker in 2005-2011: mostly broke, because they're fish on a heater

62296574
Just put in a little effort gay ass nigga its not hard to be better than 99% of people, I think im even top 2-3% earner or at least close to it but then you need to realize thats just the start its wild how much some people actually make, couple million is nothing.

62296579
>>62296456
kek'd

the average age for someone working and investing in index funds to become a millionaire is well past 60.

62296588
>>62296579
As I said above, expect the age of any forum loudmouth, even on /biz/, to be between 50 and 70; then, it makes sense

If it sounds like a boomer, it's probably a boomer

62296592
>>62295508
200k is not that crazy. Anyone can have that if they go to college, get a 100k job and live with their parents for a few years.

62296646
>>62295508
Switzerland is richer than the US

62296656
>>62296588
yep, if you're offended by people disagreeing with you in your hugbox but love to come online and say retarded things all day, you will probably encounter some loudmouths and boomers. Old people don't give a fuck about your fragile feelings.

62297538
>>62296149
Here's a (You) and a Jeffy. I won't let your hard work go unnoticed.

62297541
>>62295508
This is me. Must suck to be a Europoor or a cryptocuck.

62297556
>>62296149
intredisting

62297559
>>62297541
Pwease... anon. Pwease help a nigguh out.

62297566
>>62297541
You are part of the 0.1 . possible to ha've earned it through specific and hard work

62297591
>>62297566
In America, this places me not quite in the top 1%. Need to get to 14 million for top 1%.

Got a tech job in 2011. Six figure income plus six figure equity compensation package. Get a promotion and yearly pay increases and bonuses along the way. Keep expenses low, invest more than 70% of income per year and keep stock as the RSUs vest. Max out tax deferred investment accounts.

Enjoyed a few good bull markets in FAGMAN stocks, 2017 crypto bull, Covid bull run, and AI/GPUs/memory.

62297638
Ignoring the larpers who claim they made trillions from the quajillion market or whatever the answer is that retardditors don't have a culture of being a bagholding dumbass/schizo permabear who fuds up and coming projects or stocks for literally years on end while they're running up. The sheer amount of seethe right now at, say, HYPE which is up about 11x from its launch a year and a half ago demonstrates how gay and stupid this board is. The seething at Micron for literally 5 of the last 6 months, until it DECISIVELY crossed 1 trillion and then everyone claimed they "had calls on it" (no proofs provided). Because of the way /biz/ is you can have the same dumb faggots spamming fud or their dumb baggie/bear threads every day endlessly while reddit's threads exist, are selfcontained and people with usernames and an agenda can be ignored . They simply aren't able to constantly spam unfounded fud and basically fuck the entire board out of making it on a good coin or stock because they've got a dumb short that they think they can make if they spam their stupid shit often enough, or a bag they're married to.

Ironic. /biz/ culture of chaos originally enabled new projects to spring up and be shilled rapidly but this has no become a source of weakness, while not being able to be a spamming permabear dumbass has enabled redditors to accumulate good projects and make actual money.

>>62297591
sneed

62297869
>>62297638
I invested in micron like 4 months ago off advice from here and am officially in the black on my portfolio

62297883
Reddit users are typical SF cucks working tech, with salaries of 200k+ and stock options in an insane tech boom its hard NOT to be a millionaire after a few years

62297890
>>62295508
Most of Europe has gotten poorer. If you have your shit together the US is a far better place to be.

>Statistics show that the median savings in top wealth western nations is like 50-80k

Yeah, are you such a brainlet to not recognize that's the median and that the guy who wants to brag about his savings and focuses on finance might be a 1 in 20 guy?

High paying jobs in the US just don't really exist in Europe. A 24 year old lawyer in big law is going to be at $250,000 after bonus for his starting pay and making probably close to $500k by 30, if he makes partner he'll be earning $10MM+ a year. An AI engineer will make $500k-100MM+ a year. Surgeons will make $1M-$10MM in a lot of fields and locations.

Lots of Americans don't earn that or blow their money on pick-up trucks, but the European mind cannot comprehend American prosperity

62297936
>>62297890
The tradeoff is having to live here and not comfy eurolands
always grass (appears) greener

62298065
>>62295508
>How is it that everyone on reddit are millionaires
Have you not considered reddit is a communist marxist psyop? You've seen how controlled they are over there and how anything said thats negative gets down voted into oblivion, plus anything thats promoting anything thats outside the leftist/progressive narrative gets banned right away and shut down.

The reason why theres a lot of money floating around over there is because half the place is leftist marxist spooks who are bankrolled to condition and push people were they want them to be: Digital

62298085
>>62297566
>source from 2015

62298922
>>62295508
If you weren't part of the top 10% in 2025, you're lower class

62299401
>>62298085
alright man heres current data. not much changes

62299510
>>62298922
That 10% includes household equity. Liquid millionaires are closer to 2%, and yes that does matter.

62301411
>>62296458
> stocks go up, zoomers stay poor. crypto's dead, move on.

item.title: The people who get ahead at work lie, cheat and steal — 20 replies — 3.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
The people who get ahead at work lie, cheat and steal

Open thread • Replies: 20 • Updated: 2026-06-02 03:41 UTC

OP

https://youtu.be/tg6eHzEH3Nk
>lied on resume about college and military service
>gets CIA agent job
>requests 666.6 pounds in gold bars for “work related expenses”
Everyone I know who is great at getting jobs is a lying thief. These people have no problem shaking your head while looking you in the eyes and lie to you but they always get hired quickly

Early replies (first 3)

62295153
>>62295129
>newbie doesn't know about the charisma stat
grim

62295162
>>62295129
>this news story about government corruption is how I know what corporate life is like

62295170
>>62295129
He got caught, stupid. Cheaters never prosper.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62295220
>>62295162
Ive worked for corporations and govt and they’re exactly the same
It’s all nepotism and politics. 10% of the workers do 90% of the work no matter the place you retarded bitch
>>62295170
>chesters never prosper
I would sleep you in the face for saying something so stupid if I were around you
You have no idea how many have gotten away if 1 gets caught
The cia admitted they needed the fbi because the nature of the cia males it impossible to audit

62295231
>>62295220
Thanks, Rashmeet. I think I'll go eat a steak just to piss you off.

62295263
>>62295170
he just got scapegoated, retard
it's the work related injury equivalent of the elite class
just freak accident type shi

62295265
>>62295129
>going to prison is getting ahead
okay

62295319
>>62295263
This doesnt follow basic logic. Try again

62295341
>>62295319
I'm not here to teach you how to think, animal.

62296355
>>62295129
everything is a scam, everyone is a scammer.

62296374
>>62295341
Based Jews, let the goyim deal in the real world as a white man

62296699
>>62295129
Why the fuck would you stay in the US after stealing that much? Same shit as those kids who stole $250m in crypto and spent it on partying instead of running to a different country

62296709
>>62295220
> 10% of the workers do 90% of the work no matter the place you retarded bitch

Yep and what’s worse the hard workers get no upwards mobility and only the popular people that mostly pretend to work but take credit for everything and are joke around with the higher ups get promoted. Normies were a mistake, autists need to be in positions of power because we would only grant upwards mobility to people who actually deserve while Normies are all nepotistic bastards

62299417
>>62295129
false
cia would never lie

62300129
>>62295129
>3 letter agencies created bitcoin/crypto
>Secretly stealing gold

62301114
>>62296699
Should’ve drove that fortune to a private plane airport and ran to canada

62301119
>>62295231
Cool I had bacon this morning faggot
Indians don’t post here you stupid Israeli bitch

62301155
>>62301119
Without fail, the indian reveals his shit-covered hand.
Oh, and the steak was delicious. Part of the reason why everyone laughs at your demon gods is that you deify a stupid, slow, delicious animal and don't even eat it.
Its like worshipping a tomato.

62301205
>>62301155
Damn you glowies reply fast

62302063
>>62300129
They’re going to liquidate that $40 million buddy

item.title: I think a /biz/ group chat would be chill. — 14 replies — 3.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
I think a /biz/ group chat would be chill.

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-06-02 05:12 UTC

OP

I think a /biz/ group chat would be chill. For discussion of our picks in stocks/crypto and so on. A manageable amount of people, like 20-100. Would be nice to avoid people having random arguments about nothing every other thread and the never ending LINK posters.

Only issue is keeping scammers and stupid people out of there and not sure which platform is fitting. Only rules: be nice. Billions must smile

Early replies (first 3)

62301815
sorry i dont speak hindi

62301849
>>62301815
Unironically thought about checking IP addresses to keep out scammers but Indians can just use VPNs so everyone would have to dox themselves which isn’t really all that great. Think there’s way less Indians here now that the board is so dead anyways

62301928
>>62301812
/biz/ is a chaat group

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62301935
>>62301928
It kind of is but a group chat is just a different social environment which I think could be nice

62301938
>>62301812
crypto clown club on tg
its full of incels, fakecels, spics, and a transgender cameroonian frenchwom*n named Akane

62301946
>>62301849
must post a white/asian hand with timestamp every hour or get kicked from the chat lmao.

62301954
>>62301946
We'll figure something out

>>62301938
Sounds amazing bro I bet I’ll find the next gem there

62301957
>>62301954
they found NOCK early...

62301969
>>62301957
Never heard of it desu. Taking a quick look at the chat it seems like the thing I would want to avoid, a lot of off topic bullshit so you would miss the next big thing if someone posted about it anyways

62302003
>>62301812
isn't that basically what coindashy is?

62302042
>>62302003
Tf is that

62302147
>>62302042
This website: https://coindashy.com/
has an anon chat and crypto charts, it was made by some guy from /biz/

62302164
>>62302147
Ever found anything good there?

62302250
>>62302164
The graphics are kind of cool looking I guess? If you mean has the chat ever informed me of some unknown coin that was about to moon, then no.

item.title: who are these types of homes built for? >$390k to share a wall with a stranger >HOA fee exists but isn't disclosed on li… — 40 replies — 3.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
who are these types of homes built for? >$390k to share a wall with a stranger >HOA fee exists but isn't disclosed on li…

Open thread • Replies: 40 • Updated: 2026-06-01 21:30 UTC

OP

who are these types of homes built for?
>$390k to share a wall with a stranger
>HOA fee exists but isn't disclosed on listing
>no trees, no privacy shrubs, living there will put you on SSRIs
>undoubtedly built by drunken illiterate retard spics in 3 weeks
>costs 20% more than a SFH existing build (with trees, a lawn, NO SHARED WALLS, etc.)
you'd need to be the world's biggest goy retard to consider these.

Early replies (first 3)

62298165
for people dealing in the real world

other questions?

62298175
>>62298161
>no trees, no privacy shrubs,
If only the buyer could add those himself later, choosing whatever he prefers.

Oh well, sadly that's impossible.

62298195
>>62298161
everything about these is absolutely dreadful. it feels like you're living in an apartment (except you're on the hook for $390k over 30 years + HOA + property taxes + tip.)
>GRAY, GRAY, GRAY
>layout designed by someone with downs syndrome + FAS
yeah uh i want a single basin sink island that's barely large enough to fit a cutting board and i want it in the middle of my OPEN CONCEPT gaynigger living room + kitchen (where is the dining room? who the fuck knows) with a view of my barren wasteland front yard.

WAIT -- I'M NOT DONE -- give me a bunch of ugly ass light fixtures and drywall features to cover the oversized beam that supports the open concept ceiling. and i DO NOT want crown molding on my ugly ass gray walls because that would add too much to the cost.

oh, and can you make the stairwell barely wide enough to fit an adult? i want it to be FUCKING IMPOSSIBLE to move furniture or appliances in or out of my GOYBOX.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62298247
>>62298222
Let OP cook, this is the best thread I’ve seen on biz in weeks.

62298249
have the people who designed these types of "homes" ever heard of ceiling fans? i've never once seen a ceiling fan in a new build.
>umm actually you're just supposed to run your chinese factory seconds Heat Pump™ 24/7 until it blows up 3 hours outside the warranty period from being deliberately undersized to save on build costs.

62298255
>>62298242
do you have a learning disability?
https://www.realtor.com/realestateandhomes-search/Pennsylvania/type-single-family-home/55p-hide/price-na-400000/sby-6?view=map&pos=40.437769,-75.548192,40.302008,-75.410677,13&qdm=true

62298258
>>62298161
please americans do not build semis they are disgusting inhuman domiciles and they plague they uk housing market
i can tell you that there are people, vile subhuman philistines, who actually like having a semi because they consider it neighbourly but those people ought to be put in camps where they can enjoy a "neighbourly" dormitory

62298260
>>62298255
I don't know why becasue I don't care to look into it but there's a reason that it's more expensive.

62298263
Jeets

62298273
>>62298242
mmm, yes, yes, i see -- but these single family homes don't appeal to my goyish sensibilities. where's the smell of ethnic food seeping through my paper thin walls that i share with a brown family of 6? and all of this... what do you call it, grass and trees? i'm expected to mow that myself???

62298310
>>62298260
yes anon, materials and labor purchased today are notoriously cheaper than material and labor purchased 30 years in the past. excavating and paving dozens of acres of raw land and running hundreds of feet of water/sewer to a public tie-in is also cheaper than buying on a plot of land already tied into public utilities with a much shorter run.
>YEAHH UHH I WANT A DRIVEWAY.... BUT LIKE, I WANT IT BARELY LARGE ENOUGH TO PARK A SUBCOMPACT CROSSOVER.

62298384
not having trees is a plus... don't have to deal with cleaning leafs in the falls and don't have to deal with bird shit and tree sap messing up your car paint

i can tell none of you faggots have ever had to rake leaves

62298404
>>62298384
>not having to do 2 hours of work a week 3 months out of the year is worth living in a barren wasteland
no thanks, I’ll keep my trees

62298408
>>62298384
GOY spotted. raking leaves is a mentally ill suburban boomer phenomena. i'm puzzled by the scenario in your goybrain where trees are apparently only to be planted in areas that hang over your car? also why is your car not parked in the garage? (i know the answer. it's because you're a mentally ill suburban boomer. your garage isn't for parking vehicles, it's packed floor to ceiling with 1500 square feet of worthless nicknacks and other retarded garbage.)

62298416
The people that buy these are plebs who know nothing about basic construction/being handy, and associate anything "new" as being superior. True, there is no lead or asbestos, but the place will be falling apart within 20 years, and it is inhuman to pay that much to live in such close proximity. Homes from the 60s-70s have much better quality materials and larger lot sizes.

62298431
>>62298384
>>62298408
>OH YEAH, A RAKE? AND A LAWNMOWER? WHERE AM I GONNA KEEP THAT? I GOT NO ROOM IN MY GARAGE.
>LISTEN -- LISTEN, I KNOW IT'S CALLED A ONE "CAR" GARAGE BUT.... THAT'S JUST AN EXPRESSION!!!! A FIGURE OF SPEECH!!!!!

62298446
>>62298384
>>62298408
>>62298431
>don't you -- don't you FUCKING touch that motorcycle tire!!!!!
>it is NOT garbage!!!!!

62298546
>>62298195
>>62298207
>>62298273
>>62298310

OOOOOOO He's priced out!

Don't feel bad rentoid, I own multiple properties and also would not buy that Overpriced HOA shit.

There's a point that the younger generation has to realize what the boomers did. DIY while being totally unqualified to do it, ignore all the faggy permitting and regulations, build your own if you own the land.

62298565
>>62298546
who said i'm priced out? i make $150k and i'm looking at homes that cost $350-400k. i work from home so i have a lot of flexibility.

62298583
>>62298161
This thing looks either a made up fake computer graphic or ikea the house

62298588
read OPs posts with Nick Rochefort's voice

62298787
>>62298565

If true, wtf are you so close to Niggerdelphia?

Need easy access to ur trans hookers?

Real estate has always been about the location dawg.

62298816
>>62298310
>>62298161
These are european style shitboxes why the fuck are you having them?

62298828
>>62298787
niggerdelphia is an hour away. i might be LAID off one day, so i want to stay close to jobs that pay more than $16/hour.

62299240
Buying a house that has been made within the last 15 years is retarded. Guaranteed to have many problems.

62299332
>>62298161
Peak goybox.

62299341
>>62298175
If it is indeed part of a HOA, it is impossible

62299373
>>62298583
It says TO BE BUILT you fucking retard, yes it's been created on a computer

62300682
>>62298249
>it's either ceiling fans or heat pump
The... what? Ever heard of AC?

62300745
It's all optimized to make the most profit for the developer if you dont build your own house or buy something old.

62300830
>>62298161
They make them with shared walls because it results in a contiguous garden, instead of two small slices of yard with a house in the middle. It makes the small plot seem as if it has an okayish yard.
It's also slightly more energy efficient, so you save $3.50 on the montly energy bill

62300911
>>62298249
Why would you want a ceiling fan unc fr fr no cap

62300928
>>62300830
the question should be is it built with sound proofing in mind? if built cheaply answer of course no but you absolutely can design a house with that in mind so even feral shooting niglets couldn't be heard
